According to Theorem 4.4, \(h^{-1}(f(x+h)-f(x))\to f'(x)\) \(a.e.\) on \([a, b]\text{.}\) For each \(h > 0\text{,}\) \(h^{-1}(f(x+h)-f(x)) \) is a non-negative function in \(\cR[a, b]\) (extend \(f(x)=f(b)\) for \(x > b\)), and
\begin{equation*} \int_{a}^{b} h^{-1}(f(x+h)-f(x)) \, dx =h^{-1}\left( \int_{b}^{b+h} f(x)\,dx - \int_{a}^{a+h} f(x)\, dx\right) \le f(b)- f(a), \end{equation*}
using \(\int_{a}^{a+h} f(x)\, dx\ge f(a) h\text{.}\) Thus by Fatou Theorem,
\begin{align*} \int_{a}^{b} f'(x)\, dx \amp = \int_{a}^{b} \liminf_{h\to 0+} h^{-1}(f(x+h)-f(x)) \, dx\\ \amp \le \liminf_{h\to 0+} \int_{a}^{b} h^{-1}(f(x+h)-f(x)) \, dx \le f(b)-f(a). \end{align*}
